steps.title: Find the subject
steps.content: Strip the parenthetical: "behavioral economists such as Gee, whose research focuses on charitable giving, ____ that..." The subject is behavioral economists — plural.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Rule violated by wrong choices: subject-verb agreement — a plural subject requires the base (plural) form of the verb, not a singular inflection.
  • "has contended": singular present-perfect verb — disagrees with the plural subject "behavioral economists." ✗
  • "contends": singular present-tense verb (third-person -s) — disagrees with the plural subject. ✗
  • "is contending": singular present-progressive verb — disagrees with the plural subject. ✗
  • "contend": base-form plural present-tense verb — agrees with the plural subject "behavioral economists." ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
